caption:
Over the last year, our photographers documented stories across every continent. With more than 2.3 million images captured, we selected the 20 most compelling to feature in our Pictures of the Year 2024. 📸

From 50-foot penguin cliff jumps and elusive tigers to volcano eruptions and an eclipse, view these and other special moments featuring rare wildlife, scientific discoveries, and human exploration at the link in bio.

There's a reason why the Mediterranean diet is so popular—it works.

Packed with nutritious foods like whole grains, fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins, this diet has long been associated with a lower risk of chronic diseases. But why does it work so well? And how can it help you live a longer, healthier life?

Find out more about the Mediterranean diet and its associated health benefits at the link in bio.

Photograph by @catherinekarnow

Photo by @joelsartore | Aside from humans, gelada baboons are considered the world’s most terrestrial primate species. Living in the high mountain meadows of Ethiopia, these monkeys are grazers, spending most of their day sitting down and plucking herbs and grasses to eat. At night, geladas will climb up steep, rocky cliffs and select a ledge to sleep on, huddling together with other members of the troop until morning. This photo was taken at Parco Natura Viva in Bussolengo, Italy. #PhotoArk

Elected in 1976, Jimmy Carter left his mark on history when he became the 39th President of the United States. Before that, however, he was a beloved resident of Plains—a charming small town in Georgia. When Nat Geo photographer Jodi Cobb paid a visit during Carter's election campaign, it wasn't hard for her to see just how his hometown had shaped his life and the promises he offered the American people. 

With never-before-published images, discover the town that shaped Jimmy Carter and the excitement over the success of their hometown son at the link in bio. 

Photograph by @jodicobbphoto

There’s a lot for stargazers to look forward to in 2025.

Over the next 12 months, you may be able to see meteor showers, unusual planetary alignments, eclipses, and more. Many of these celestial spectacles will be easily visible without any specialist equipment, meaning there are sights for amateurs and experts alike to appreciate.

For a guide to the must-see night sky events of 2025, head to the link in bio.

Photographs by @babaktafreshi

Explorers and adventurers have long been fascinated by the secrets of Loch Ness. But once the myths and the legends unravel, what's really hiding in the 700ft depths of this Scottish loch? 🐉 #DrainTheOceans is now streaming on @DisneyPlus.

Read more about the mystery of the Loch Ness Monster at the link in our bio.

Thinking about taking part in Dry January? 

It might only be a month, but going sober for just a short while can have a huge impact. Within weeks of giving up alcohol, not only does the liver begin to regenerate, but you may notice improved mood, healthier skin, better sleep, and several other health benefits. A lot can change in a month—and it could be for good.

Head to the link in bio to learn how else decreasing your alcohol consumption can boost your health and why it works.

Photograph by Victoria Jones, PA Images/Getty Images

Photo by @beverlyjoubert | A backward gaze across the Kenyan plains. It's a long, dangerous, and often brutal road to becoming a predatory ruler in these parts. From their very first year, male lions face the toughest of survival odds: Only about 1 in every 8 male cubs will make it to adulthood. Whether from rival carnivores, injuries, hunger, or even the threat of their own kind as incoming males attempt to take over prides, the dangers are unrelenting. Later, there are high-risk stretches of nomadic life when maturing lions are expelled from their natal pride to seek their own luck. Behind every battle-seasoned male is a story of resilience and the sheer willpower it takes to endure. To see more of my work, follow me @beverlyjoubert.

Stop scrolling! Take 30 seconds and give them to the top students at Puppy Kindergarten 🐶

Head to the link in bio to learn how to raise a great dog.

New studies show that the volume or thickness of certain brain regions expand during monthly periods. “Through these studies, we now have this picture emerging of how potent these hormones are for shaping, not just brain morphology but also the functional architecture,” says Emily Jacobs, a neuroscientist at the University of California, Santa Barbara. 

Head to the link in bio to learn what we do—and don’t—know about how the menstrual cycle changes the brain.

Photograph by Dr P. Marazzi, Science Photo Library

From their Pleistocene ancestor to their buns of steel, we've got the full scoop on what makes wombats so fascinating 🐾 Find out more about them—including why their poop is cube-shaped at the link in bio.

Our best wildlife photos from 2024 will take you on a journey across the planet—from monarch butterflies in Mexico to bears in Romania, each image is a testament to the warmth and wonder of our world. See them all at the link in bio. 

Photographs by Jasper Doest (@jasperdoest), Jaime Rojo (@jaimerojo), Nichole Sobecki (@nicholesobecki), Brian Skerry (@brianskerry), and Tim Laman (@timlaman).

There are few natural phenomena as captivating as eternal flames—but how do they work?

Found globally—from New York to Taiwan to Australia—an eternal flame is exactly what it sounds like: a fire that burns both continually and autonomously, sometimes for hundreds or even thousands of years.

They’ve inspired myths and legends for generations. Nowadays, they regularly go viral on social media, but how is it that they can burn for as long as they do?

Learn more about the science behind eternal flames and where you can find them at the link in bio.

Photograph by Jakob Fischer, Alamy Stock Photo

Are you up for a volcanic adventure? 🌋

📍 Guatemala

Have you ever heard of the term “psychofauna?” Similar to the giant sandworms in the movie "Dune," some of Earth's animals can also produce mind-altering chemicals 🪱 Find out more about them at the link in bio.

Host: @sarahgibbens

Believers call him the Son of God. Skeptics dismiss him as legend. In recent years, researchers digging in the Holy Land have been sifting fact from fiction.

Both believers and skeptics claim archaeology as their ally. What have we learned of the historical Jesus? Discover more at the link in bio.

Photograph by @simonnorfolkstudio

Photo by @amivitale | Glacial milk flows into Glacier Lake within Nahanni National Park Reserve in the Northwest Territories. This stunning phenomenon occurs as fine-grained, silt-size rock particles, formed by the grinding of bedrock through glacial erosion, mix with meltwater, resulting in a distinctively milky appearance. The scene is nothing short of magnificent.

To reach the park, I traveled by floatplane, watching the rugged beauty of the Northwest Territories unfold below—a mesmerizing patchwork of steep mountains and glacial lakes. Up here, the chill feels distant, but once we land, we become part of this wild tapestry. Nothing happens in isolation; we are all included in this collective heartbeat of life around us.

Equal parts spectacle and serenity, Italian monasteries are evolving to stay relevant in modern times. Offering reflection, introspection, and breathtaking scenery, visitors of all backgrounds are welcomed and encouraged to stay, transforming these ancient sites into the perfect base for a peaceful getaway. Find out more about how these monks are adapting their monasteries at the link in bio.

📍Abbey of Monte Oliveto Maggiore
📍Sacra di San Michele

Videos by @andrea_frazzetta and @lucanestola_

A snow day always makes for a memorable moment, but in Finland, the ice and snow freeze into unusual shapes, creating a real-life winter wonderland. 

Head to the link in bio to learn more about photographer Stian Klo's journey to photograph this surreal scenery.

Photographs by Stian Klo (@stianmklo)

Photo by @joelsartore | Scientists don’t know much about the elusive northern Luzon giant cloud rat, named for the Philippine island where it’s found. The animal dwells mainly in the upper tree branches of lowland tropical and montane rainforests, from sea level up to the high mountains. Photo taken at the Plzeň Zoo in Czechia. #PhotoArk
